src/flit/platform/locale_format

        formatCurrency
      • formatCurrency(n: float; currency: string = ""; locale: string = "en-US"): string
        formatDate
      • formatDate(t: DateTime; pattern: string = "yyyy-MM-dd"; locale: string = "en-US"): string
        formatNumber
      • formatNumber(n: float; decimals: int = 2; locale: string = "en-US"): string
        formatPercent
      • formatPercent(n: float; decimals: int = 1; locale: string = "en-US"): string
        localeFor
      • localeFor(locale: string): LocaleConfig

Locale-aware date / number / currency formatting.

Native APIs:

  • macOS: NSNumberFormatter, NSDateFormatter
  • Linux: ICU (libicu) or the C strftime / setlocale
  • Windows: Windows.Globalization.NumberFormatting

This module ships a pure-Nim implementation that handles the common cases (English, German, French, Japanese number / currency separators; ISO-8601 dates). Real CLDR coverage would mean shipping the CLDR data or binding to ICU.

Types

LocaleConfig = object
  locale*: string
  decimal*: string
  thousands*: string
  currency*: string
  currencyBeforeAmount*: bool
Per-locale separators and currency settings. Returned by localeFor. Pass a custom LocaleConfig to extend beyond the built-in locales. 
NumberFormat = enum
  nfDecimal, nfPercent, nfCurrency
What kind of number a value represents. Drives which formatter to call (formatNumber, formatPercent, formatCurrency). Exposed for users building dynamic formatter dispatch on top of this module.

Procs

proc formatCurrency(n: float; currency: string = ""; locale: string = "en-US"): string {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Formats n as a currency amount in the given (or locale's default) currency. Default 2 decimal places, except JPY which traditionally shows none. 
proc formatDate(t: DateTime; pattern: string = "yyyy-MM-dd";
                locale: string = "en-US"): string {.
    ...raises: [TimeFormatParseError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Formats a date with the given pattern. Patterns follow Nim's times format directives; locale-aware month / weekday names are not implemented (Nim's format uses English; full CLDR locale data would need shipping). 
proc formatNumber(n: float; decimals: int = 2; locale: string = "en-US"): string {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Formats a number with the locale's decimal and thousands separators. decimals is how many fractional digits to show. 
proc formatPercent(n: float; decimals: int = 1; locale: string = "en-US"): string {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Formats a fraction in [0, 1] as a percentage. 0.456 → "45.6%". 
proc localeFor(locale: string): LocaleConfig {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Returns the LocaleConfig for a BCP-47 locale string. Accepts both dash and underscore forms ("en-US" / "en_US"). Falls back to en-US when the locale is unknown.

Built-in locales: en-US, en-GB, de-DE, fr-FR, ja-JP, hi-IN. Extend by constructing your own LocaleConfig and passing it directly to the formatters that take one.
